title: Sieve of Eratosthenes
code:
primes :: [num]
primes = sieve [2..]

sieve :: [num] -> [num]
sieve (p:xs) = p : sieve [x | x <- xs; x mod p ~= 0]

first_n_primes :: num -> [num]
first_n_primes n = take n primes
description: The Sieve of Eratosthenes as a lazy infinite list. Miranda's `[2..]` generates naturals from 2, and list comprehensions with `;` for guards filter out multiples. Lazy evaluation ensures only requested primes are computed.
